name: Watchtower Observation Post
description: The elevated guard post above the death strip — a steel and concrete structure with a 360-degree observation window, a telephone that connects directly to the command post, and a mounted searchlight that has been left on its fixed position all evening. From here, the entire checkpoint is visible: the crowd on the eastern side, the press on the western side, the death strip below.
atmosphericDetails: The searchlight's beam cutting through the fog at a fixed angle, illuminating a cross-section of the crowd that shifts and breathes like a single organism
The telephone handset on the observation desk — its cord slightly frayed at the connection point, its line to command post still technically operational
The view from above: the death strip's raked gravel, the two crowds on either side, the bridge, and in the distance the lights of West Berlin glowing amber through the fog
